implemented: `maskfactory daz backup create --tier A` plans by default and writes only with `--apply`; source and destination are explicit and no D-drive default exists.
`maskfactory daz backup verify` checks the self-seal, exact file set, hashes, policy, Tier-A identity, and SQLite integrity read-only.
`maskfactory daz backup restore-test` plans by default and copies only with `--apply` into an empty isolated target.
Create and restore plans validate all required categories without creating destination or target directories.
A non-empty restore target is rejected with stable code 77 and its existing bytes remain unchanged.

validation.regression_command: python -m pytest -q tests/test_daz_backup.py tests/test_daz_monitoring.py tests/test_daz_storage.py tests/test_daz_scheduler.py tests/test_daz_control_plane.py tests/test_daz_runtime_worker.py

remaining_acceptance: Configure a real backup destination on a separate present volume or Kevin-selected private backup system.
Run a drained live Tier-A create/verify/clean-root restore drill.
Rebuild registry views and execute one exact recipe semantic replay, package verification, and dataset/model lineage query against the restored root.
Record duration, missing-file result, path remapping, and final evidence hashes before completion.
claim_boundary: This proves the dry-run-safe command surface and isolated byte/hash/SQLite restore fixtures only. It does not select a real backup destination, copy live F-drive state, execute semantic replay or lineage recovery, re-enable maintenance, or complete MF-P9-12.04.
